name: smart-expense-tracker
description: A smart expense management web app with categorized transactions, recurring expenses, budgets, analytics charts, multi-currency support, and CSV/JSON export.
requirements:
## Requirements (EARS Format)

### R1: Transaction Management
When the user adds a new transaction, the system SHALL record the amount, category, date, description, and currency.
When the user edits a transaction, the system SHALL update all modified fields and persist changes.
When the user deletes a transaction, the system SHALL remove it from the database and update analytics.

### R2: Category Management
The system SHALL provide default categories: Food, Transport, Housing, Entertainment, Shopping, Health, Education, Other.
When the user creates a custom category, the system SHALL persist it with a name and optional icon/color.

### R3: Recurring Expenses
When the user marks a transaction as recurring, the system SHALL store the recurrence pattern (daily, weekly, monthly, yearly).
The system SHALL auto-generate upcoming recurring transactions on schedule.

### R4: Budget Management
When the user sets a monthly budget for a category, the system SHALL track spending against that budget.
When spending exceeds 80% of budget, the system SHALL display a warning.
When spending exceeds 100% of budget, the system SHALL display an alert.

### R5: Analytics & Charts
The system SHALL display spending breakdown by category as a pie/donut chart.
The system SHALL display monthly trends as a line/bar chart.
The system SHALL show daily, weekly, monthly, and yearly summaries.

### R6: Multi-Currency Support
When the user selects a currency for a transaction, the system SHALL store the currency code (ISO 4217).
The system SHALL support at least: USD, EUR, VND, JPY, GBP.
The system SHALL convert amounts to a base currency for analytics using stored exchange rates.

### R7: Data Export
When the user clicks Export CSV, the system SHALL generate and download a CSV file of filtered transactions.
When the user clicks Export JSON, the system SHALL generate and download a JSON file of filtered transactions.

### R8: Search & Filter
When the user types in the search box, the system SHALL filter transactions by description in real-time.
The system SHALL support filtering by date range, category, and currency.
design:
## Design

### Architecture
- Frontend: React 19 + Vite + TailwindCSS + Recharts
- Backend: Express.js + SQLite (better-sqlite3)
- API: RESTful JSON endpoints

### Data Model
```mermaid
erDiagram
  TRANSACTION {
    string id PK
    number amount
    string currency
    string category
    string description
    string date
    boolean isRecurring
    string recurrencePattern
    string createdAt
    string updatedAt
  }
  CATEGORY {
    string id PK
    string name
    string color
    string icon
    boolean isDefault
  }
  BUDGET {
    string id PK
    string categoryId FK
    number limit
    string month
  }
  EXCHANGE_RATE {
    string fromCurrency PK
    string toCurrency PK
    number rate
    string updatedAt
  }
```

### API Endpoints
- GET /api/transactions - List with filters
- POST /api/transactions - Create
- PUT /api/transactions/:id - Update
- DELETE /api/transactions/:id - Delete
- GET /api/categories - List all
- POST /api/categories - Create custom
- GET /api/budgets - List all
- POST /api/budgets - Set budget
- GET /api/analytics/summary - Summary stats
- GET /api/analytics/by-category - Category breakdown
- GET /api/analytics/trends - Monthly trends
- GET /api/export/csv - Download CSV
- GET /api/export/json - Download JSON
- GET /api/currencies - List supported currencies
